skill.name: libreoffice-writer
skill.description: Create, edit, format, export, and inspect LibreOffice Writer documents through UNO-backed Python helpers.
skill.summary: Automates LibreOffice Writer document creation, editing, export, patching, and page snapshots.
skill.author: dfk1352

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found most are false positives from Markdown code formatting, UNO property names, and document-targeting terms. The confirmed risks are legitimate but elevated: the skill runs a local LibreOffice process, modifies caller-specified files, creates temporary profiles, and can extend the UNO import path from an environment variable. No evidence found of prompt injection, network exfiltration, credential theft, or malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Local LibreOffice Process Launch
medium_findings.description: The skill starts a local LibreOffice executable with subprocess.Popen to provide UNO document automation. The call uses an argument list and no shell interpolation, so command injection was not found, but marketplace users should understand that the skill launches external desktop software in headless mode.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess launch is directly present and semantically required for the skill. Confidence is high for external process execution, while command injection risk is lower because shell=True is not used and arguments are fixed.

medium_findings.title: Caller-Controlled Document File Writes
medium_findings.description: The skill creates, stores, exports, snapshots, and edits documents at paths supplied by the caller. This is expected for an office automation skill, but it can overwrite or create files where the running agent has permission if prompts choose unsafe paths.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem write behavior is clear and intentional across create, export, and snapshot functions. Confidence is medium-high because the risk depends on how the host agent constrains user-selected paths.

medium_findings.title: Environment-Controlled UNO Import Path
medium_findings.description: The UNO bridge reads LIBREOFFICE_PROGRAM_PATH and inserts candidate directories into sys.path before importing the UNO module. This supports legitimate LibreOffice setups, but a hostile environment could influence which UNO module is loaded.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The environment variable and sys.path insertion are directly visible. Confidence is medium because exploitation requires control of the process environment or filesystem, but the import-path trust boundary is real.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Command Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Many external command alerts come from Markdown code spans and examples that document Python APIs, shell setup, or patch syntax. They are not executable code by themselves and do not create command injection risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are documentation examples or fenced blocks, not runtime code. Confidence is very high that these static hits are scanner noise.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high weak-cryptography alerts map to ordinary document metadata, target keys, names, and UNO property handling. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, password hashing, encryption, or signature verification code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the flagged contexts found document fields and property names rather than cryptographic operations. Confidence is very high because no crypto API use appears in these locations.

low_findings.title: Temporary Profile Directory Cleanup
low_findings.description: The UNO bridge creates a temporary LibreOffice profile directory and removes it after the process exits. This is expected isolation behavior, with residual low risk if cleanup fails during abnormal termination.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Temporary directory creation and cleanup are explicit. Confidence is high that the behavior is safe by design, with only routine local cleanup risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Process Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: Uses subprocess.Popen to launch LibreOffice headless for UNO automation. This should remain shell-free and restricted to trusted LibreOffice binaries.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The process launch is confirmed. The risk is bounded by fixed arguments and absence of shell invocation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local File Mutation
dangerous_patterns.description: Writes Writer documents, exports, and PNG snapshots to local paths selected by the caller.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file writes are core functionality. The danger depends on host path permissions and user prompt control.

dangerous_patterns.title: Import Path Modification
dangerous_patterns.description: Adds LibreOffice candidate directories, including an environment-provided path, to sys.path before importing UNO.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The path modification is confirmed. It is a legitimate compatibility feature with environment-trust implications.

content.value_statement: Manual Writer document edits are slow and hard to repeat. This skill lets Claude, Codex, and Claude Code automate ODT creation, structured edits, exports, and snapshots through LibreOffice UNO.
actual_capabilities: Create new LibreOffice Writer ODT documents at absolute paths.
Open session-based editing workflows for text, tables, images, and lists.
Apply structured patch operations with atomic or best-effort behavior.
Format matched text with font, size, color, alignment, and spacing options.
Export documents to PDF, DOCX, or page-level PNG snapshots.
Read and update common Writer document metadata fields.
limitations: Requires LibreOffice and Python UNO support on the local machine.
APIs expect absolute file paths for Writer-facing operations.
Text targets can fail when anchors are ambiguous or too short.
Snapshot sizing assumes A4 page dimensions in the current implementation.

use_cases.title: Prepare Business Reports
use_cases.description: Create formatted ODT reports, update tables, insert action lists, and export final versions to PDF or DOCX.

use_cases.title: Revise Existing Documents
use_cases.description: Use anchored targets to replace text, apply formatting, update images, and keep repeated document edits consistent.

use_cases.title: Validate Document Layouts
use_cases.description: Capture PNG snapshots of specific pages to inspect layout after automated formatting or content changes.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Document
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a LibreOffice Writer document at an absolute path with a title, three short sections, and a bullet list.

prompt_templates.title: Edit Targeted Text
prompt_templates.prompt: Open this Writer document and replace the paragraph after the Executive Summary heading with the revised text I provide.

prompt_templates.title: Format and Export
prompt_templates.prompt: Format the financial summary as centered bold text, update the summary table, then export the document to PDF.

prompt_templates.title: Apply a Multi-Step Patch
prompt_templates.prompt: Build and apply a Writer patch that replaces draft wording, updates one table, inserts an image, and reports each operation result.

output_examples.input: Create a quarterly report with a heading, financial summary, and action list.
output_examples.output: A new ODT document is created with structured sections, formatted summary text, and a nested action list.

output_examples.input: Update the Summary table and export the Writer file as PDF.
output_examples.output: The named table is updated, the document is saved, and a PDF copy is written to the requested path.

output_examples.input: Capture page 1 of the document for layout review.
output_examples.output: A PNG snapshot is generated with width, height, DPI, and output path information for review.

best_practices: Use absolute paths and confirm output locations before creating or exporting files.
Use distinctive sentence-level anchors with after and before bounds for reliable targeting.
Prefer atomic patch mode when partial document changes would be unacceptable.
anti_patterns: Do not target common single words when several matches may exist.
Do not run the skill without a trusted LibreOffice installation and UNO module path.
Do not export or snapshot to broad system directories unless the path is intentional.

faq.question: Does this skill require LibreOffice?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses LibreOffice UNO and starts LibreOffice in headless mode for document work.

faq.question: Which document formats can it export?
faq.answer: The exposed export helpers support PDF and DOCX. The snapshot helper exports a selected page as PNG.

faq.question: Can it edit existing ODT files?
faq.answer: Yes. It opens existing Writer documents and edits text, tables, images, lists, metadata, and formatting.

faq.question: How does it choose where to edit?
faq.answer: It uses structured targets with text, after, before, occurrence, name, or index fields depending on the content type.

faq.question: Is it safe for untrusted paths?
faq.answer: Use caution. The skill writes to caller-selected paths, so host permissions and path review are important.

faq.question: What should I do if UNO import fails?
faq.answer: Install LibreOffice Python UNO support or set LIBREOFFICE_PROGRAM_PATH to the LibreOffice program directory.
